authority.canonicalHeadPolicy: The first merge parent is the live PR #252 head resolved exactly once at the same atomic prepare/start transition that resolves the base. SHAs recorded in these documents are authoring-time observations; later accepted commits on the branch supersede them without a new router revision.

phaseGate.releaseCondition: The broker pushes and proves a reviewed ordered two-parent merge for the attempt-bound live base, with GitHub reporting PR #252 non-conflicting at that same head and base.

invariants: ProjectScopedControl reads the live PR #252 base exactly once while atomically preparing and starting one product attempt.
The resolved base is a full exact 40-hex commit SHA; a symbolic ref, branch name, abbreviated SHA, prior observation, or packet-authored value is invalid.

canonicalHeadSha, materializationSourceSha and orderedParentShas[0] all equal the live PR #252 head resolved once at the atomic prepare/start transition; routerAuthoritySha records the router authoring-time head.
The product worktree is materialized from the live-resolved canonical head, never from a historical product-wave ancestor or the resolved base.
The exact resolvedBaseSha is orderedParentShas[1] and can never be re-resolved or substituted inside the attempt.
The runtime performs the ordered mechanical merge materialization before worker start and records its exact actual conflict paths in the same pre-start contract.
ProjectScopedControl selects and freezes the exact focused test commands after conflict discovery and before worker start.
Any later live-base inequality invalidates only this productAttemptId and all of its output; the stable packet and format remain valid for a new atomic attempt.
At most one product attempt is active, under review, or eligible for promotion at a time.

runtimeOwnsOnly: subscription-runtime builtin worker-start-v1 atomic admission/start primitive
merge-commit construction, promotion and push primitives
controllerOwns: the complete DAG and all dependency ordering
live-base resolution timing and attempt admission
worker count, role, scope and replacement decisions
mechanical gate selection and pass/fail evaluation

mandatoryReadOrder: the exact attempt-bound conflict paths and their nearest focused tests

preserve: accepted-integrated Phase 2 product-wave behavior inherited by canonicalHeadSha from historicalAcceptedProductWaveSha
requiredBeforeResult: all focused tests selected for every actual conflict path
nodes.resultStorage: immutable runtime-owned result keyed by productAttemptId

nodes.required: ProjectScopedControl invokes runtime execution primitives and directly evaluates the complete mechanical gate set against the producer's exact resolved tree.

nodes.resultStorage: immutable runtime-owned review result keyed by productAttemptId and accepted tree SHA

requiredProof: merge commit has exactly the ordered two parents
merge tree equals the accepted reviewed tree
push uses canonicalHeadSha as the expected old PR head
remote PR head equals the merge commit after push
GitHub PR #252 head OID equals the merge commit and base OID still equals attempt.resolvedBaseSha
GitHub mergeability is resolved and non-conflicting for that exact head/base pair

productMechanicalGates: binding schema, exact-40-hex values, canonical source, ordered-parent and attempt-ID equality
fresh live-base comparison with attempt.resolvedBaseSha; inequality invalidates the attempt
exact actual-conflict scope and byte equality for every mechanically merged non-conflict path
zero unmerged index entries and zero unresolved conflict markers
all focused tests selected for every conflict path and both parent behaviors
pnpm lint:fast:files -- <exact changed TypeScript and TSX conflict paths>
pnpm exec prettier --check <exact changed text conflict paths>
exact diff, binary, symlink, secret, credential, provider/auth payload and private/real-project path classification

dimensions: both-parent behavior preservation in every conflict resolution
integration coherence across the complete resolved tree
Clean Architecture, DDD, SOLID and documented feature-boundary compliance
security, trust-boundary, path, auth/provider and data-exposure regression review
focused-test adequacy and consistency with controller mechanical evidence
absence of whole-side selection, unrelated cleanup, scope widening or concealed behavior deletion
semanticReview.acceptance: One fresh independent reviewer returns ACCEPT with P0/P1/P2 0/0/0 for the exact attempt ID, base SHA and accepted tree SHA.
semanticReview.rejection: No promotion; only the controller may decide whether to admit a bounded replacement attempt under this same router.

comparisonPoints: immediately before broker merge construction and push
during post-push GitHub head/base conflict proof

provenancePolicy.acceptedTreeBinding: Git tree SHA plus productAttemptId in runtime-owned records

phase2PacketDisposition.currentAuthority: this execution index and its exact six companion router paths

forbidden: durable packet pinning of an observed PR base SHA
old same-job continuation or dirty-worktree reuse
product edits outside exact actual conflict paths
Phase 2 packet edits by this router
one-parent, squash, patch-only, reversed-parent or whole-side merge promotion
repository handoff manifests or manifest-hash provenance
product terminal, smoke, provider, auth or raw lifecycle activity
other repositories, broad documentation work or Fast mode
runtime DAG selection or successor authorization
